Bug #149708: `flutter pub upgrade` reports outdated, but `flutter pub outdated` reports nothing

### Steps to reproduce

1. `flutter upgrade`
2. Create new Flutter project
3. `flutter pub upgrade`. Notice:
> 8 packages have newer versions incompatible with dependency constraints. Try `flutter pub outdated` for more information.
4. `flutter pub outdated` Note report that 1 package (the lints package) is outdated.
5. `flutter pub upgrade --major-versions`
6. `flutter pub upgrade`. Notice repeat of "8 packages have newer versions" message
7. `flutter pub outdated` Get:

Showing outdated packages.
[*] indicates versions that are not the latest available.

Package Name  Current  Upgradable  Resolvable  Latest  

direct dependencies: all up-to-date.

dev_dependencies: all up-to-date.
all dependencies are up-to-date.


### Expected results

`flutter pub upgrade`, `flutter pub upgrade --major-versions` and `flutter pub outdated` should agree about what's going on.

### Actual results

See steps to reproduce

### Code sample

### Expected results

The "id" parameter should stay the same as before, the tab path should change (navigationShell.currentIndex)

### Actual results

The Id stays the same, but the nested path won't change

Bug #149800: CanvasKit FragmentShaders do not update uniforms when reusing a Paint object.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Have some kind of Fragment shader that uses a uniform to show something graphically.
2. Inside a paint() function, create a new Paint object that uses that shader and cache it.
3. Also in the paint function, if there is a cached version of the Paint object, use that instead of making a new one.
4. Update the uniforms of the shader
5. Notice how in the cached version's paint, the changed value is not reflected in the render

### Expected results

Each time you update a uniform via setFloat and friends, the value should be reflected in the shader's uniform.

Bug #149833: Flutter iOS module is not building when using firebase_app_check package

### Steps to reproduce

Just follow these steps:

1. Create a new Flutter module: `flutter create --template module angled_error`
2. Install the package firebase_app_check: `flutter pub add firebase_app_check`
3. If installing the latest version of firebase_app_check (0.3.0), change the iOS deployment target to version 13.
4. Run or build the module for iOS: `flutter run` or `flutter build ios-framework`

Tested in Flutter 3.19.1 and 3.22.1. Failing in both.
Tested with Firebase App Check 0.2.2+7 and 0.3.0. Failing in both.

The error only happens in a Flutter module project. In a Flutter app project it works as expected.

It seems like the Flutter module iOS project is missing some configuration that the app iOS project has. Of course this is just a supposition.

If I need to move the issue to the Firebase project, I will not have any problem to do it.

### Expected results

The expected result will depend of the command you are trying to run.

If you are running the project, the result should be the module being run on the simulator or iOS device.
If you are building the module, the result should be the build artifacts.

### Actual results

The actual result is the next error:

> Lexical or Preprocessor Issue (Xcode): 'FLTAppCheckProviderFactory.h' file not found with <angled> include; use "quotes" instead
/some/path/.pub-cache/hosted/pub.dev/firebase_app_check-0.3.0/ios/Classes/FLTFirebaseAppCheckPlugin.h:10:8
>
> Parse Issue (Xcode): Could not build module...

text:
Error messages:
The actual result is the next error:

Team response: Hi @AriasBros 
This should be fixed on the plugin side. The current version of `firebase_app_check` uses `<angle>` include instead of quotes, see https://github.com/firebase/flutterfire/blob/master/packages/firebase_app_check/firebase_app_check/ios/Classes/FLTFirebaseAppCheckPlugin.h#L5-L13.

For reference on a similar fix for 1st party package `image_picker`: https://github.com/flutter/plugins/pull/5635/files.

Meanwhile, you can try a workaround from this [answer](https://stackoverflow.com/a/63648164/5921933).

Please report the issue on [flutterfire](https://github.com/firebase/flutterfire) repository for better support there. Closing the issue. If you disagree, please write in the comments and I will reopen it. Thanks!

Bug #149866: Assets path is different depending on compilation mode on web.

https://pub.dev/packages/flutter_markdown gets images differently depending on platform and on build mode.

Markdown:
```
...
click the ![import]($uploadImageUrl) button
...
```

uploadImageUrl:
```
  const isWebProd = kIsWeb && !kDebugMode;
  const imagePath = isWebProd ? 'assets/' : '';
  final uploadImageUrl = '${imagePath}assets/img/doc/upload_$filePostfix.png';
```

Relates SO post: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/62292512/firebase-flutter-web-app-works-locally-but-when-deployed-images-are-not-shown
 

PR in DevTools that has to do this trick: https://github.com/flutter/devtools/pull/7843/files#diff


Team response: Hi @polina-c 
I checked this using [basic_markdown_demo](https://github.com/flutter/packages/blob/main/packages/flutter_markdown/example/lib/demos/basic_markdown_demo.dart) sample code which loads an asset as `![import](assets/logo.png)` but I see the `logo.png` still is displaying on both `debug` and `release` modes. 

Can you provide more clues to reproduce this issue?

Bug #149999: Odd scroll behavior and occasional asserts with trackpad

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run flutter create and replace main.dart with the provided code sample.
2. Run on macOS
3. Do a scroll gesture over the ListView using the trackpad

### Expected results

Smooth scroll with bouncing overscroll effect
Can reverse scroll direction without releasing double tap

### Actual results

Scroll appears to jump erratically, no overscroll effect
When at viewport top, after trying to scroll up further cannot reverse scroll down by reversing the swipe direction 

Current master:
![master](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/72562119/d9422d27-6e27-4adb-ba0f-a53de04fff3a)

After reverting to 529a4d2:
![revert](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/72562119/250df0c9-b3d0-49d0-a3db-b9944464afe4)

Occasionally the following asserts are seen:

<details open><summary>Logs</summary>

```
════════ Exception caught by scheduler library ═════════════════════════════════
The following assertion was thrown during a scheduler callback:
'package:flutter/src/animation/animation_controller.dart': Failed assertion: line 898 pos 12: 'elapsedInSeconds >= 0.0': is not true.

Either the assertion indicates an error in the framework itself, or we should provide substantially more information in this error message to help you determine and fix the underlying cause.
In either case, please report this assertion by filing a bug on GitHub:
  https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/new?template=2_bug.yml

When the exception was thrown, this was the stack:
#2      AnimationController._tick...

text:
Bug #150012: [Web] ScrollController throws unexpected null exception on web, when getting maxScrollExtent 

### Steps to reproduce

Create an app with two screens - home one with a `ListView`, another with any widget.  Add check that `position.maxScrollExtent > 0` in `ScrollController`'s `onAttach` method. Launch an app and go to the second page. Refresh page and you will see `Unexpected null value` exception. There's no app crash, etc. but the exception is thrown every time.

### Expected results

No exception is thrown.

text:
Bug #150157: [webview_flutter] Third party calls by javascript sdk not working in iOS 17.5.1

### Steps to reproduce

For some reason, since iOS 17.5.1 came out, the WebView that I used to communicate dart with a payment provider through the javascript sdk started to fail most of the time the call is made.

The strange thing is that the WebView works perfectly for any other distribution from 17.5 onwards.

I have done tests using: webview_flutter: ^3.0.0, webview_flutter: ^4.8.0 and flutter_webview_pro: ^3.0.1+4 with the same result for all.

The problem is found when executing the javascript function, in which the call cannot be executed, remaining in a kind of cache. After several attempts, there are times it works and other times it finishes processing. (Confirmed since I implemented the Sentry library to monitor what happens inside the javascript)

### Expected results

Make calls synchronously to the payment provider through the javascript SDK and respond in case of success or failure

text:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create three text fields on home page
2. Create one field on second page
3. Run project in flutter web
4. Click on Second Field in home page
5. Press Enter to navigate to second page
6. Press Escape to return to previous page
7. Click on First Field in home page
8. Press F2 to navigate to second page
9. Press Escape to return to previous page

### Expected results

First Text Field should have kept its focus

**Insights that might help**

- Works as expected if KeyboardListener from Home is removed
- Works as expected if 4,5,6 steps are not followed
- Works as expected in Flutter v3.10.6

### Actual results

First Text Field loses focus
Facing this issue in Flutter web (v3.22.2)

Bug #150296: Conditionally bundling assets based on flavor make tests to fail

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new Flutter project (no need to create native flavors as this also fails with normal projects too)
2. Add a new asset image and register it under pubspec.yaml as a flavor asset
3. Create your app with an Image that references the image asset you just added to your project
4. Write your UI tests asserting the image will be there
5. Run your tests (with or without the --flavor param)
6. Tests are failing: Unable to load asset


### Expected results

1. Create a new Flutter project (no need to create native flavors as this also fails with normal projects too)
2. Add a new asset image and register it under pubspec.yaml as a flavor asset
3. Create your app with an Image that references the image asset you just added to your project
4. Write your UI tests asserting the image will be there
5. Run your tests (with or without the --flavor param)
6. Tests are passing and assets are properly rendered under UI tests

### Actual results

Unable to load assets, no matter if you pass or not the flavor parameter. 

Also, if you don't configure conditional imports under pubspec.yaml, tests work like a charm:
1. Create a new Flutter project (no need to create native flavors as this also fails with normal projects too)
2. Add a new asset image and register it under pubspec.yaml
3. Create your app with an Image that references the image asset you just added to your project
4. Write your UI tests asserting the image will be there
5. Run your tests (with or without the --flavor param)
6. Tests are passing


### Code...

Team response: Thanks for the detailed report @AngeloAvv 


> * Write your UI tests asserting the image will be there
> * Run your tests (with or without the --flavor param)
> * Tests are failing: Unable to load asset

Since you are trying to run `flutter test`, according to https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/132985, the use case you are trying to achieve currently supports `flutter run` and `flutter build` only, so it may not be applicable to `flutter test` that you can check.

Also, /cc @andrewkolos for thoughts on the issue report.

Bug #150316: `TabBar.tabAlignment` property does't work when `dividerColor` is transparent

### Steps to reproduce

I what tabs to be align at start and dividerColor is to be transparent. 

```
TabBar(
            dividerColor: Colors.transparent,
            tabAlignment: TabAlignment.start,
            isScrollable: true,
            tabs: [Tab(text: "a"), Tab(text: "b")])
```

But tabs are always at center, the property `tabAlignment ` does't work




### Expected results

I want tabs to be at start. It works when dividerColor is not set or set to any value but transparent

```
TabBar(
           dividerColor: Colors.red,
           tabAlignment: TabAlignment.start,
           isScrollable: true,
           tabs: [Tab(text: "a"), Tab(text: "b")])
```

you can see tabAlignment is set, but it does't work

Bug #150334: Menu Bar Keyboard Traversal Issues

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create Home Page with MenuBar
2. Run app on Flutter Web
3. Click on Submenu Button
4. Keyboard Traversal works initially
5. Press Escape to close menu
6. Again click on submenu button
7. Keyboard traversal stops working 

### Expected results

Keyboard Traversal (arrow up, arrow down, left, right) should work

Bug #150342: Scrollbar gesture recognizers not updated on scroll extent change

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run flutter create and replace main.dart with the provided code sample.
2. Run on macOS
3. Wait a few seconds for the text in the TextField to appear
4. Do a scroll gesture over the Text using the trackpad
5. Move the mouse pointer outside of the app window or tap on the app bar
6. Again, do a scroll gesture over the Text using the trackpad

### Expected results
text_with_context:
[BUG] #150342 | Area: regression | Status: closed | Version:  | OS: macos
Bug #150342: Scrollbar gesture recognizers not updated on scroll extent change

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run flutter create and replace main.dart with the provided code sample.
2. Run on macOS
3. Wait a few seconds for the text in the TextField to appear
4. Do a scroll gesture over the Text using the trackpad
5. Move the mouse pointer outside of the app window or tap on the app bar
6. Again, do a scroll gesture over the Text using the trackpad

### Expected results

text:
Consistent trackpad scroll behaviour, identical list of gesture recognizers.

### Actual results

Inconsistent trackpad scroll behaviour, different list of gesture recognizers.

Before text update:
```
flutter: Gesture arena 2    ❙ ★ Opening new gesture arena.
flutter: Gesture arena 2    ❙ Adding: VerticalDragGestureRecognizer#8b17a(start behavior: start)
flutter: Gesture arena 2    ❙ Closing with 1 member.
flutter: Gesture arena 2    ❙ Default winner: VerticalDragGestureRecognizer#8b17a(start behavior: start)
```

After text update:
```
flutter: Gesture arena 4    ❙ ★ Opening new gesture arena.
flutter: Gesture arena 4    ❙ Adding: VerticalDragGestureRecognizer#8b17a(start behavior: start)
flutter: Gesture arena 4    ❙ Adding: _VerticalThumbDragGestureRecognizer#293c1(debugOwner: _MaterialScrollbarState#10516(tickers: tracking 2 tickers), start behavior: down)
flutter: Gesture arena 4    ❙ Adding: _VerticalThumbDragGestureRecognizer#eacad(debugOwner: _MaterialScrollbarState#1f937(tickers: tracking 2 tickers), start behavior: down)
flutter: Gesture arena 4    ❙ Closing with 3 members.
flutter: Gesture arena 4    ❙ Accepting:...

It appears to work as expected on `stable 3.22.2` but the issue appears on `master`

text: Bug #150418: Using Material 3 on Android, the Status Bar color changes when using Flutter Engine Cache
text_with_context:
[BUG] #150418 | Area: android | Status: closed | Version:  | OS: windows
Bug #150418: Using Material 3 on Android, the Status Bar color changes when using Flutter Engine Cache

text:
### Steps to reproduce

We use FlutterEngineGroup and FlutterEngineCache in our application.
This is because we are incorporating Flutter screens into an existing native application.

The steps to reproduce the issue are as follows:
1. Launch the application.
2. Perform initialization processing in the Android app.
3. Call up the Flutter screen using FlutterEngineGroup and FlutterEngineCache.
4. Exit the Flutter screen with the back key. The cache is not discarded in this case.
5. Launch the Flutter screen again using the cached FlutterEngine.

When you perform the above operations, the color of the Status Bar will change.

### Expected results

The status bar color should remain the same.

### Actual results

If you discard the cache and start a new engine this problem doesn't seem to occur.

### Code sample
We will prepare a minimal sample that will allow you to confirm the phenomenon.

<details close><summary>Code sample in Flutter</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: Colors.orange),
        useMaterial3: true,
      ),
      home: const MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({super.key, required this.title});

  final String...

Team response: Thanks for the detailed report. I was able to replicate the reported behavior with provided details in latest versions.

<details>
<summary> stable, master flutter doctor -v </summary>

```

    en-GB)

      /Users/dhs/documents/fluttersdk/flutter
    ! Warning: `flutter` on your path resolves to
      /Users/dhs/Documents/Fluttersdk/flutter/bin/flutter, which is not inside
      your current Flutter SDK checkout at
      /Users/dhs/documents/fluttersdk/flutter. Consider adding
      /Users/dhs/documents/fluttersdk/flutter/bin to the front of your path.
    ! Warning: `dart` on your path resolves to
     ...

2. `cd build/web`

text: Bug #150549: [camera_android] 0.10.9+5 breaks the preview's orientation ### Steps to reproduce 1. `camera_android: 0.10.9+5` 2. Runs on a real device. 3. Choose the back lens. 4. The preview got rotated unexpectedly. Bug #150638: TextPainter getFullHeightForCaret returns confusing values with nested TextSpans

### Steps to reproduce

Paste the following code snippet into dartpad.dev, main channel (Dart SDK 3.5.0-266.0.dev and Flutter SDK 3.23.0-13.0.pre.242):
```dart
import 'package:flutter/painting.dart';

void main() {
  final painter = TextPainter(
    text: TextSpan(text: 'M', style: TextStyle(fontSize: 128), children: [
      TextSpan(text: 'M', style: TextStyle(fontSize: 32)),
      TextSpan(text: 'M', style: TextStyle(fontSize: 64)),
    ]),
    textDirection: TextDirection.ltr,
  );
  painter.layout();
  print(painter.getFullHeightForCaret(TextPosition(offset: 0), Rect.zero));
  print(painter.getFullHeightForCaret(TextPosition(offset: 1), Rect.zero));
  print(painter.getFullHeightForCaret(TextPosition(offset: 2), Rect.zero));
  print(painter.getFullHeightForCaret(TextPosition(offset: 3), Rect.zero));
}
```

Click Run. Observe the printed output:
```
150
150
150
150
```


### Expected results

I expected to see the caret height change according to the style of the span "owning" the text position. For example, I might expect values more like:

```
150
150
37.5
75
```

because these correspond to the font size of the span directly preceding the offset. I think there could be a debate about whether the painter should look to the upstream or downstream span to retrieve the style, but either way I would not expect the caret height to be the same at all positions in this example.

text:
### Actual results

Caret height reported as the same value, 150, for each offset in the text painter.

Bug #150652: [camera_android_camerax] `ResolutionPreset.max` gets different camera aspect ratio compare to `camera_android`

### Steps to reproduce

1. Setup the example of `package:camera`.
2. Update the `ResolutionPreset` for the `CameraController` to always use `max`.
3. Switch between `camera_android` and `camera_android_camerax` implementation.
4. The aspect ratio of all cameras is different.

#### Reproducible Devices
- OnePlus 11 (PHB110)

### Expected results

They might have the same specs.

### Actual results

All cameras have different aspect ratios between implementations.

Bug #150761: SVG rendered with aliasing when using colorFilter property on Impeller.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the code sample with `flutter run  --no-enable-impeller`. The original and filtered SVGs are rendered correctly.
2. Run the code with `flutter run`. The original SVG is rendered correctly, while the filtered version has visible aliasing.

The example is using flutter_svg: 2.0.10+1

Tested on physical and simulator iOS device.

The SVG used in the reproduction:
![example](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/30197200/5ae144d8-de15-4f6f-93eb-56938427d036)

Bug #150853: colorFilter not working in Flutter Web Apps on mobile devices with iOS 17.2 on Safari anymore

### Steps to reproduce

Using 

SvgPicture.asset(pathToAsset, colorFilter: ColorFilter.mode(Color(0xFFFF0000), BlendMode.srcIn));  

... tints the asset red on iOS 15 on Safari on mobile devices but not when mobile device is running on safari on iOS17.2 it does not change the color.

Bug was opened in flutter_svg more than 2 weeks ago without a reaction by developer:

Bug #150902: [Add-to-App] [Android] Flutter Engine on Android still active after FlutterActivity get disposed

### Steps to reproduce

I have an issue with Flutter engine created from FlutterEngineGroup is still alive when navigating back from Flutter view back to native Android view. The engine that used with previous FlutterActivity is still active. This happen when I provide custom engine from EngineGroup with function **provideFlutterEngine**, when I use the implicit engine created by default from FlutterModuleActivity, the engine gets destroyed. 

### Expected results

When navigating back from Flutter to native, it should stop the engine.

### Actual results

Please check the provided sample code.

Bug #150911: [material/menu_anchor.dart] Hovering over menu item affects scrolling and focus order.

### Steps to reproduce

3. Hover over item 50 and remove your hand from the mousepad.

Bug #151029: iOS VoiceOver accessibility regression: EditableText rotor actions only available when keyboard is focused

After the changes implemented in issue #94465, there seems to be a regression in VoiceOver accessibility for text editing on iOS.
While I cannot be certain that this issue directly caused the problem, I noticed the regression after this change was implemented, suggesting a potential correlation.
This issue significantly impacts the user experience for visually impaired users.

Current behavior:
1. When a user activates an editable text field and the keyboard appears, they must first focus on the keyboard with VoiceOver before using the rotor to access editing options like "Select All" and "Paste".
2. If VoiceOver focus is on the text field instead of the keyboard, using the rotor to navigate to "Edit" only shows "Scan Text" option.

Expected behavior (previous functionality):
Users could immediately use the VoiceOver rotor to access editing options like "Select All" and "Paste" after activating the text field, without needing to focus on the keyboard first.

This change makes it much more difficult for visually impaired users to perform common text editing tasks. Previously, they could easily paste text using the rotor immediately after activating the text field. Now, they must first navigate to the keyboard, which can be problematic, especially if the keyboard input mode is set to "Touch Typing". In this mode, any touch on the keyboard immediately activates keys, potentially inserting unwanted characters.

To reproduce:
1. Enable VoiceOver on an iOS device.
2. Run the following minimal example:

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void...

Bug #151029: iOS VoiceOver accessibility regression: EditableText rotor actions only available when keyboard is focused

After the changes implemented in issue #94465, there seems to be a regression in VoiceOver accessibility for text editing on iOS.
While I cannot be certain that this issue directly caused the problem, I noticed the regression after this change was implemented, suggesting a potential correlation.
This issue significantly impacts the user experience for visually impaired users.

Current behavior:
1. When a user activates an editable text field and the keyboard appears, they must first focus on the keyboard with VoiceOver before using the rotor to access editing options like "Select All" and "Paste".
2. If VoiceOver focus is on the text field instead of the keyboard, using the rotor to navigate to "Edit" only shows "Scan Text" option.

Expected behavior (previous functionality):
Users could immediately use the VoiceOver rotor to access editing options like "Select All" and "Paste" after activating the text field, without needing to focus on the keyboard first.

This change makes it much more difficult for visually impaired users to perform common text editing tasks. Previously, they could easily paste text using the rotor immediately after activating the text field. Now, they must first navigate to the keyboard, which can be problematic, especially if the keyboard input mode is set to "Touch Typing". In this mode, any touch on the keyboard immediately activates keys, potentially inserting unwanted characters.

To reproduce:
1. Enable VoiceOver on an iOS device.
2. Run the following minimal example:

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void...

Feature Request #134228: Support Interactive Media Ads (IMA) in Flutter (Android & iOS)

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Flutter app owners/developers want to monetize their video content with in-stream video ads in line with IAB's VAST/VMAP specifications. Google's [Interactive Media Ads SDK](https://developers.google.com/interactive-media-ads) is a popular client library choice for publishers to do so in line with industry standards. Currently, there is no officially supported Flutter IMA plugin.




### Proposal

Requesting official Flutter support for IMA SDKS on iOS & Android platforms. Initial request is to support the client side architecture of IMA SDK.

Community support: 8 reactions (8 upvotes)

Status: shipped/closed

I have a `TableView` in a screen with an app bar that does not have a straight edge. Wrapping the `TableView` with a padding widget causes artifacts with the scroll glow.

![Screenshot_20230911-203251](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/48153628/22dde1ae-69fa-4df2-867d-25961c847536)

### Proposal

`TableView` from the two_dimensional_scrollables package should have a `padding` attribute like any other scrollable widget that allows to add padding around the content without shrinking the scrollable area.

@Piinks

Feature Request #134501: Allow an embedded Flutter Web view to size itself Copied from internal bug b/298531093 I'm integrating an embedded Flutter Web into a Google product. One of its limitations is that the Flutter view can only size itself to fill its viewport contents and not vice-versa. For example, if I attach a Flutter view to this div:
Then the Flutter view will be (correctly) constrained to 100x100, but if I don't specify the div's dimensions:
The Flutter will assume that the viewport size is 0x0. The expected behavior, at least for me, would be for the viewport to take the size reported by the Flutter view. Right now I can sort of get away with having a fixed height and variable width, but I run into problems when the browser window is resized (see b/298531093 for details). For certain kinds of embedded views, especially small views with dynamic content, it is difficult and a poor user experience to give the content a fixed size before rendering. Ideally, it would be possible for Flutter to render and resize it's DOM element depending on the outcome. I'm not sure how this feature request interacts with the actual rendering mechanics or... Status: shipped/closed

text: Feature Request #134539: Update CupertinoActionSheet design ### Is there an existing issue for this? - [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues) - [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports) ### Overview The current implementation of CupertionActionSheet differs a little bit from the design referenced in the HIG, [Apple Design Resource](https://www.figma.com/community/file/1248375255495415511). According to my observation, this is because the thickness of the border line separating the actionSheetItem, the fontSize for the title and description, and the spacing for the title and description sections are different. text:
Feature Request #134570: Enforce (or at least warn for) the min supported Android version

We currently don't have any enforcement of the minimum sdk that we support (as defined in our [supported platforms](https://docs.flutter.dev/reference/supported-platforms)). 

Despite [increasing the minimum from 16 to 19](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/82000), a fresh flutter app edited to have minSdkVersion 15 provides no error or warning. We should consider enforcing the minimum, or at least providing a warning.

We could also maybe use this to provide a warning 1 stable cut ahead of when we drop support for a version range, though I'm not sure that noise would be desirable.

Status: shipped/closed

We could also maybe use this to provide a warning 1 stable cut ahead of when we drop support for a version range, though I'm not sure that noise would be desirable.

Feature Request #134577: Provide ability to determine if text was autofilled vs manually entered.

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Currently, the Flutter AutofillGroup lacks the ability to differentiate between user-inputted data and autofilled content, such as, in customized password input fields. This deficiency becomes problematic when developers incorporate a "show password" feature, introducing a potential security vulnerability. When autofill is employed, in conjunction with the "show password" functionality, it inadvertently exposes the password.

If an individual inputs their credentials and grants the browser permission to save this information, a subsequent user accessing the application from the same browser can exploit autofill to access the saved username/password combination. This user can then click on the "show password" option, thereby exposing the password.

```
AutoFillGroup(

  // Username
  child: TextField / TextFormField(
  ...
   autofillHints: const [AutofillHints.username, AutofillHints.email, AutofillHints.newPassword],
  ...
 ...

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #134776: [flutter_markdown] Add a way to change the default `MarkdownStyleSheet` down the widget tree

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Sometimes it's helpful to change the theme for the entire subtree, like you can do with the `Theme`, `DefaultTextStyle` or `IconTheme` widgets. It would be helpful to have the opportunity to do the same for the styles of `MarkdownBody` (changing `MarkdownStyleSheet`).

### Proposal

```dart
DefaultMarkdownStyleSheet(
  style: MarkdownStyleSheet(
    p: TextStyle(color: Colors.orange)
  )
  child: Card(
    child: MarkdownBody(
      data: "My text" // this text is orange
    ),
  ),
);

text:
Feature Request #135250: Update base ref from main->master on PRs opened to flutter/flutter

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing infra issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3Ateam-infra)

### Type of Request

feature request

### Infrastructure Environment

Cocoon Scheduler

### What is happening?

It would be nice if cocoon automatically detected whether you are merging to the correct default branch, and if you aren't have it automatically change to the correct one. This recently was an issue in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/135097, where the base branch being merged to was `main`, which resulted in not all of the targets being run. Once we changed the base to `master`, all of the targets ran per usual.

### Steps to reproduce

1. I create a fork in `flutter/flutter`
2. I make a change in my fork and commit it
3. I open a PR, with the base set to `main`.
4. (Feature request) Cocoon recognizes that I am trying to merge to `main` and changes the base to `master`.

### Expected results

The PR I created is merging to the default branch, `master`.

Status: shipped/closed

- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Create a simple way for something like a `ListView` to be pinch-zoomed. This is a somewhat common pattern with a list of vertical images. If the images contain text then a user may want to zoom the entire list itself rather than each image when the user gets to it. Two concrete examples I can think of are a comic reader and reviewing a list of documents before submitting them. text:
Feature Request #135530: Support multi-view rendering

In order to make the Flutter engine's rendering system support multiple views, we need to change multiple components across the engine. The plan can be found in design doc [flutter.dev/go/multi-view-pipeline-and-rasterizer](http://flutter.dev/go/multi-view-pipeline-and-rasterizer). Items are listed below. The order of the items does not represent dependency, and some items can be developed in parallel.

- [x] **Item 1:** Eliminate violations of the rendering rule (https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/45300)
- [x] **Item 2:** Make the pipeline support multiple views. The `Animator` should collect (instead of dispatching) the layer tree in `Render` and dispatch them at the end of `BeginFrame` (https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/44473).
- [x] **Item 3:** Make the `Rasterizer`'s structure compatible with multiple views. (https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/45512)
- [x] **Item 4:** Allow the `ExternalViewEmbedder` to handle multiple views. (https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/51722, https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/51719, https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/46169)
- [x] **Abandoned:** Allow the `RasterCache` to handle multiple views. 
  - Per @/stuartmorgan...

Community support: 5 reactions (5 upvotes)

text:
Feature Request #135574: Expose `onAttach` and `onDetach` in `PageController` constructor

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

The new `onAttach` and `onDetach` in `ScrollController` are currently not usable with `PageController` since its constructor doesn't include them, and extending classes can't set the fields on `ScrollController`

As discussed in #124823:

>This resolves a long held pain point for developers. When using a scroll controller, there is not scroll position until the scrollable widget is built, and almost all methods of notification are only triggered when scrolling happens. Adding these two methods will help developers gain access to the scroll position when it is created. A common workaround for this was using a post frame callback to access controller.position after the first frame, but this is ripe for issues such as having multiple positions attached to the controller, or the scrollable no longer existing after that post frame callback. I think this can also be helpful for folks to debug cases when the scroll controller has multiple positions...

Status: shipped/closed

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

> Please tell us the problem you are running into that led to you wanting
a new feature.

We have a vertical list of `ExpansionTile`'s, and would like the ability to disable user interaction on the `ExpansionTile`, while simultaneously allowing it's `children` to still be clickable. In our case, each `ExpansionTile` contains a grouping of cells, and we want the `ExpansionTile` that contains the currently selected item to be locked open. All of the other `ExpansionTile`'s in the list should be able to be expanded or collapsed; only the `ExpansionTile` with the currently selected item should be locked open. Currently, disabling toggling an `ExpansionTile` is not supported.

Feature Request #135848: [flutter_markdown] please allow custom block elements

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I tried to add my own custom block element using a `blockSyntaxes: const [NoteSyntax()],` declaration in the `Markdown` widget. My `NoteSyntax` creates an `Element('note', children)` which then eventually gets passed to the visitor that creates widgets.

I tried to add my own builder using `builders: {'note': NoteBuilder()},` in the `Markdown` widget, but this is never called because `MarkdownBuilder.visitElementBefore` uses a private function `_isBlockTag` to determine whether the provided `Element` is a block element by comparing its tag to a hardcoded list of tags. 

Therefore, it incorrectly thinks my `Element` is an inline element and crashes [^1] because `_addParentInlineIfNeeded` wants add an inline element that requires a `tag` but the parent of my block node is the document which has no tag.

[^1]: See line 632 of `builder.dart`, `style: styleSheet.styles[tag!],`.

### Proposal

I'd suggest to dispatch the `isBlock` call to the...

Feature Request #135873: ScaleUpdateDetails should give sourceTimeStamp

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Similar to https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/43833. We should be able to get the `sourceTimeStamp` being that you can do so in onPanUpdate like so:
```dart
onPanUpdate: (DragUpdateDetails details){
  print(details.sourceTimeStamp);
}
``` 
My specific use case involves converting a scale event to a vertical drag event which requires `sourceTimeStamp`. A perhaps more reasonable use case would be to track the velocity of a "pan" event (meaning a pan event from a scale event) in eg. `InteractiveViewer` using a `VelocityTracker`, which requires a `time` in its `addPosition` method. This could be useful when eg. trying to detect when a user wants to close the `InteractiveViewer`.

### Proposal

I'm not sure where `sourceTimeStamp` comes from. It doesn't seem to be used in `gesture_detector.dart` so I don't know where to add it. If scale really is a superset of pan I imagine it should not be difficult to add it. If anyone knows...

My specific use case involves converting a scale event to a vertical drag event which requires `sourceTimeStamp`. A perhaps more reasonable use case would be to track the velocity of a "pan" event (meaning a pan event from a scale event) in eg. `InteractiveViewer` using a `VelocityTracker`, which requires a `time` in its `addPosition` method. This could be useful when eg. trying to detect when a user wants to close the `InteractiveViewer`.

### Proposal

I'm not sure where `sourceTimeStamp` comes from. It doesn't seem to be used in `gesture_detector.dart` so I don't know where to add it. If scale really is a superset of pan I imagine it should not be difficult to add it. If anyone knows...

Status: shipped/closed

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Hi there,

As mentioned in this issue: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/120046#issue-1571549369 from @elliotnash and a previous experience of mine. Sometimes running Flutter run / build with a "broken" image will result in to a error on iOS. When finding this specific image and "fixing" it (re-adding / new illustrator export etc.) it will work.


### Proposal

What I would like to suggest (if possible) to show in the logs **which** image has failed to codesign. For example, I have added like 40 images to my project, while debugging and it all worked.

But now running it in release it will crash..., so I know I need to find the specific image causing this, but it would be nice if the logs would give me exactly which image is causing the problem.

text:
Feature Request #136113: Support deferred components on desktop platform(MacOS/Windows/Linux)

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case
1. On the desktop side, we want to achieve code dynamic based on different interfaces which rely on the implementation of deferred components, so we can only globally update app. so, we cannot only update a certain part.
2. We have two different flutter process and two `app.so` in one application. We want use deferred components to reduce app size by sharing the same compiled file(`flutter framework` compile separately)

Related:
- https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/79327. 

### Proposal
Deferred components was implemented for Android in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/57617. We should support similar functionality on desktop(MacOS/Windows/Linux).

1. Provider APIs like `LoadDartDeferredLibrary` and `LoadDartDeferredLibraryError` and `RequestDartDeferredLibrary` on `embedder.h`
3. Support platform code in shell/platform/(darwin|windows|linux)
4. Add build tools params to build part aot files.

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #136157: RawAutocomplete: Add a call to hide suggestion box

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I'm creating an app with a lot of Textfields on a single page. Most of them show Autocomplete options with RawAutocomplete. When the Autocomplete drop-out is open, the user is unable to really navigate the page. The user is unable to close the Autocomplete without the textfield losing focus or selecting an option:

![image](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/109872040/dd10295b-60da-4c6d-a80e-eb98eb4c593f)


### Proposal

There should be an option to hide the drop-down in-app. The options should re-appear when the user starts typing again.

Looking at the code, there are only four criterias defined: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/901077bf0ed5c4280cc749c6906c85f42b356b6c/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/autocomplete.dart#L327-L329

1. `_userHidOptions` is true
2. Textfield is in focus
3. Nothing is selected
4. There are options available

Criteria 3 & 4 are used for the normal function of Autocomplete and not relevant...

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #136314: Platform Isolate

This is a tracking bug for the Platform Isolate feature. The design details are still being worked out, but the general idea is to provide a way of using FFI to invoke native APIs that can only be be called from the platform thread.

Some related bugs: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/113099, https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/28310, https://github.com/dart-lang/sdk/issues/52106, https://github.com/dart-lang/sdk/issues/38315, https://github.com/dart-lang/sdk/issues/42570, https://github.com/dart-lang/sdk/issues/46943, https://github.com/dart-lang/native/issues/463

Remaining work tasks after initial PR landed

- Allow calling `runInPlatformThread()` from helper isolates: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/150584
- In `PlatformIsolateManager::RemovePlatformIsolate` and `PlatformIsolateManager::ShutdownPlatformIsolates`, find a way of asserting that we're on the platform thread: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/150586

Community support: 14 reactions (14 upvotes)

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #136621: Add the ability to clip shapes other than `RoundedRectangle` in `ExpansionTile`

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Currently, the `ExpansionTile` does not clip the `RoundedRectangleBorder` (a common use case) in either its expanded or collapsed state. This issue has been addressed in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/135855.

However, implementing a similar fix for other shapes, such as the `StadiumBorder` and `BeveledRectangleBorder`, is more complex. A comprehensive solution is needed within the `ShapeBorder` implementation that can handle border radii for all shape types. This would eliminate the need to check for every shape with a border radius within the `ExpansionTile` widget. It's also worth noting that the `StadiumBorder` doesn't even provide access to its border radius.

### Proposal

Add the ability to clip shapes in `ExpansionTile` other than `RoundedRectangle` when such a method exists in `ShapeBorder`.

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #136706: TapRegion should detect right click or middle button click

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I am currently having difficulties to detect if a mouse right click or mouse middle click has happened outside of a widget. This is really necessary to display a custom menus in a desktop application.

Currently, TapRegion widget only detects left mouse click for onTapInside and onTapOutside. Thus, there is no way currently to detect if a right click or middle click event has happened outside of the region of a widget. On the other-hand, GestureDetector can detect right click etc but no way to detect if the click has happened outside of the GestureDetector.

### Proposal

It would be really nice to have TapRegion detect all kinds of mouse button click event.

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #136811: [pigeon] Add a protocol for Flutter APIs in the Swift generator

In https://github.com/flutter/packages/pull/5159 we needed a binary messenger mock, which makes the test fragile to future changes to that class, and also dependent on some Pigeon internals. What we really want there is a mock of the FlutterApi that we've defined within the plugin, which would be more robust and easier to use. However, in Swift we can't mock without having a protocol.

For Flutter APIs, instead of generating just the class, we should generate a protocol with the methods defined in the Pigeon definition, and then the current class as implementing that protocol. Then unit tests can use an exposed-for-test initializer that passes in a mock API implementation (with the real init calling that helper with the current `IosQuickActionsFlutterApi(binaryMessenger: messenger)`), and there won't need to be a mock binary messenger at all.

Status: shipped/closed

For Flutter APIs, instead of generating just the class, we should generate a protocol with the methods defined in the Pigeon definition, and then the current class as implementing that protocol. Feature Request #136943: Disable option for SearchBar

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Sometimes, a user interface element is inappropriate/not applicable for the current program conditions and it would be confusing to entire hide this element. The best alternative is being able to disable the element. Flutter provides this capability for some widgets by passing null to the onPressed.

[SearchBar](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/SearchBar-class.html) is a widget utilized by some third party plugins such as [simple_search_dropdown](https://pub.dev/packages/simple_search_dropdown). I can find no way to disable its widget. However, since it utilizes searchbar, it would be good if searchbar could provide an "enabled" parameter. Passing false to this widget would cause it to ignore mouse clicks, tabbing into it and have it display with a grayed out look to let the user know it's disabled. 

### Proposal

Explained above.

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #137251: Implement similar widget to``AnimatedDefaultTextStyle`` but for child ``Icon``

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I have a ``Row``with a ``Text``and an ``Icon`` as children. I'm using ``AnimateDefaultTextStyle`` to animate the color change and the fontWeight change on the text, but I want to apply that same color animation that the text has, to the icon. 

### Proposal

``AnimateDefaultTextStyle`` should also affect to child ``Icon``. Based on the use case I commented, If I want to also animate the color change on the icon, apart of the text, I have to create an animation controller and the animation just for the icon, because the text does that with the ``AnimateDefaultTextStyle``.
The alternative would be to create a widget similar to ``AnimateDefaultTextStyle`` but for ``Icon``.

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #137268: `flutter run --local-engine` should generate overridden dependencies for `sky_engine` automatically.

Right now, when I modify Dart code in my local engine build and want to test those with flutter code from the flutter repo, I run something like this:

```
flutter run -d linux --local-engine-host=host_debug_unopt --local-engine=host_debug_unopt
```

The problem is that this fails to compile because the local version of sky_engine doesn't match the Dart changes I've made in the engine, and I need to add something like this to the `pubspec.yaml` of the thing I am running:

```yaml
dependency_overrides:
  sky_engine:
    path: /usr/local/home/gspencer/code/engine/src/out/host_debug_unopt/gen/dart-pkg/sky_engine
```

Making these changes now works for running the app I want to run, but the IDE still shows errors until I also add that same stanza to the `packages/flutter/pubspec.yaml` file.

Which means I need to update the override if I switch to another build type, and I need to remember to revert the pubspecs before committing.

It wouldn't fix the IDE problem, but when I specify `--local-engine` to `flutter run`, it would make sense to also transparently add the correct override to the pubspec for running the application.  It might mean that it somehow needs to...

Status: shipped/closed

Looking for **volunteers** to help with this!!! 🚀

Issues: [all](https://github.com/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3A%22a%3A+leak+tracking%22+), [open](https://github.com/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22a%3A+leak+tracking%22).

Focus areas: 
@polina-c: kick off cleanup for notGCed and [integration with testWidgets](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bXannXQ8rEtfwxEVd7LDo7xiVu5-QxRoall28MIIFss/edit#heading=h.dk8qogiupft4)


Bigger issue: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/134787

Leak tracking is now off by default for Flutter Framework.

Feature Request #137530: ExpansionTile Improvements

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

There is no problem indeed but this would be much better if the `ExpansionTile` widget supports these parameters:
* dense
* visualDensity
* reverse (if it is `true` then the tile should render beneath its children) - defaults to `false`
* enabled
* enableFeedback

> I know that we can wrap this widget with ExpansionTileTheme or even ListTileTheme to force changes but it would be much easier to have direct access to those parameters in the main widget.

### Proposal

I want to add more access to the ExpansionTile widget's ListTile and make it possible to change the arrangement of placing ListTile and the children.

text:
Feature Request #137608: [shared_preferences] Support namespaces/prefixes for multi-account apps

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I have an app that supports multiple accounts and want separate `SharedPreferences` per account.

### Proposal

If an app supports multiple accounts and wants to use separate preferences per account the current `shared_preferences` implementation does not suffice.

I suggest allowing `SharedPreferences.setPrefix()` to be allowed to be called multiple times (currently it throws an error) and to invalidate the cache each time `setPrefix()` is called.

This would allow multi-account apps to separate preferences per account.

Status: shipped/closed

Upon opening the example app's Android build.gradle, I noticed a deprecation warning for `compileSdkVersion`.
Apparently it is being renamed to `compileSdk`.

![Screenshot 2023-10-31 at 14 28 32](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/23480364/fc8ac95d-0a41-4662-a949-512e27d13c16)

IIRC this was not the case yet in Gradle 8.0 and Android SDK 33

Just filing this for visibility.

Feature Request #137683: Add support for fixed accent colors

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

[Part of #91605 ]
Material 3 has introduced a set of new color roles that retain the same color in the light and dark themes collectively referred to as fixed accent colors See: https://m3.material.io/styles/color/the-color-system/color-roles#232e7275-6ef2-4b93-afa6-c5f76e378ced

New surface color roles have also been added: (See #137679)
These changes appear to be part of a wider set of updates to the color system, see [this](https://youtu.be/vnDhq8W98O4?t=649). 


### Color Roles

#### Fixed Accent Colors
See: https://m3.material.io/styles/color/the-color-system/color-roles#232e7275-6ef2-4b93-afa6-c5f76e378ced
| Color Role         | Tone: Light | Tone: Dark |
| ------------------ | ----------- | ---------- |
| Primary Fixed      | P-90        | P-90       |
| Primary Fixed Dim  | P-80        | P-80       |
| Secondary Fixed    | S-90        | S-90       |
| Secondary Fixed Dim| S-80        | S-80       |
| Tertiary Fixed     |...

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #137721: [go_router] Add ability use query parameters during redirection with go_router

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I need to pass "from" as a query to the route. After everything I needed will be done on that route I want to redirect back to the uri from route parameter. Now it doesn't work, but I'm pretty sure it was working like this before. For example here's the logs: 
```
[GoRouter] getting location for name: "verifyPhoneNumber", queryParameters: {from: /join/hoopit-746087/open/lukas-sitt-lag-385239/roles}
[GoRouter] redirecting to RouteMatchList(/join/verify)
```
And as you can see in the second line no info about query parameters has been passed. If I missing something, please let me know, but so far seem like unreasonable restriction

### Proposal

I propose add/return ability to pass query params to redirection callback

Community support: 1 reactions (1 upvotes)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the attached demo app

I would like to have an option which disables horizontal scrolling of the text.
When i limit the width of the dropdown menu i am aware that menu item's labels can have a larger text and therefore are not fully visible.
At the moment it seems that the text is always scrollable when i drag it horizontally.
Since i have to horizontally scroll some DropdownMenu instances in a Row the scrolling of an overflowing text inside a menu at the same time is quite disturbing.

text:
Feature Request #138092: Add an event to support post-submit actions when all post-submit tests finish

It is not uncommon that we need to do post-submit actions when all tests finish. For example:
1) Packages repo needs to [waits for all post-submit tests to pass](https://github.com/flutter/plugins/blob/main/.github/workflows/release.yml#L37-L47) before publishing @stuartmorgan 
2) Engine repo needs to roll latest version to framework when all post-submit tests pass (WIP) @XilaiZhang 

We need to have a generic way to flag when all post-submit tests pass. Eg. this event can then be consumed from GitHub actions to act accordingly.

/cc @godofredoc 

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #138147: No offline check when performing various Flutter CLI steps

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Our attempts to onboard Flutter into a major bank conflicts with our regulatory control procedures. We cannot use any applications which will go out to the public Internet - our networks do not allow this. 

Installing Flutter via git repository is a non-starter.

We begin with the Flutter SDK packages [available here](https://docs.flutter.dev/release/archive), and set the developers environment up thus

* PUB_HOSTED_URL set to our internal Pub server
* FLUTTER_STORAGE_BASE_URL set to our internal Google mirror
* Remove the Git origin remote
* Disable telemetry
* Disable analytics

As we do this, the calls to disable telemetry and disable analytics have long network timeouts associated with them before the command concludes.

The runner does not accept --offline or --no-version-check

```shell
/bin/flutter -vv --no-version-check --disable-telemetry
[   +5 ms] executing: sw_vers -productName
[  +30 ms] Exit code 0 from:...

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #138171: Corporate users need a way to populate an internal mirror of FLUTTER_STORAGE_BASE_URL

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

Think of some large, highly regulated companies in the US as operating in a restricted Internet capacity (even more restrictive than China).

Our developer laptops do not have free access to the Internet, nor are we allowed to poke holes in our firewall for an exception to Google servers.

When Flutter is executed, depending on the command and the OS and chipset, Flutter will go out to a network location (base url specified by `FLUTTER_STORAGE_BASE_URL`) to download any number of support files and binaries specific to that version of Flutter. 

Within a firewall (corporate or China), we have the ability to change the base URL specified by FLUTTER_STORAGE_BASE_URL to a reachable URL for the current network. Any calls made by Flutter (and a number of other tools), will respect the environment variable to locate files.

HOWEVER, without the ability to mirror Google's default base URL for supplementary Flutter content, we...

Feature Request #138208: Gap between widgets in Column widget

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I'm from web development background and in CSS we have `gap` property in flex boxes which allow developers to maintain gap with just single line.

```css
.container {
 display: flex;
 flex-direction: column;
 gap: 12px;
}
```

i'm trying to develop a mobile app with Flutter (btw really really amazing framework) and i already searched over internet there's like `gap` feature in Flutter.

### Proposal

i know that we can do that with spaceBox widget and with some other tricks but it will be a good to have a gap feature in Flutter itself.

Thank you.

Feature Request #138430: Allow (on demand/toggle) running post-submits as pre-submits

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing infra issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3Ateam-infra)

### Type of Request

feature request

### Infrastructure Environment

I'd like to see this implemented in the code that parses and/or understands `.ci.yaml`, which, IIRC, is Cocoon?

---

Similar requests:

- @gaaclarke's https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/111418.

### What is happening?

## Background

The engine repo has insufficient capacity to run every kind of test on every kind of platform on every commit.

As a result, we run many tests as [`presubmit: false`](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/main/.ci.yaml), i.e.: [this config](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/eba757803a6f3144d4875c54db51a0add551c72c/.ci.yaml#L245-L248). That works fine for the vast majority of commits, but sometimes we are changing things that are _only_ exhaustively tested by postsubmit.

Two recent examples:

1. https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/138381; 
  due to not running the clang-tidy postsubmits.

2. https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/47397;
  caused a device lab failure due...

Community support: 1 reactions (0 upvotes)

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #138483: expose kexboardType and textInputAction parameters of the SearchAnchor TextField

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

For a new Material3 project, I am trying to implement SearchAnchor instead of SearchDelegate. 
However, in contrast to SearchDelegate, the new SearchAnchor appears to be limited in customization options. 
To allow for more customization of the search experience, it would be beneficial to expose parameters similar to the ones of the SearchDelegate. Example parameters are keyboardType or the textInputAction. 


### Proposal

introduce textinputAction and kexboardType as parameters for the SearchBar constructor. Also include them in SearchAnchor() and SearchAnchor.bar() constructors.

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #138511: [cocoon] Throttle swarming presubmit tasks during peak hours of users consuming a large amount of infra quota

Occasionally, someone will send a large number of cleanup PRs which consumes a large chunk of presubmit capacity. To protect overall SLOs for the community, we should deprioritize this user for the day.

Peak hours would be around 10 AM - 3 PM PST on US workdays.

This could be some metric based on the number of builds being scheduled. For example, we could batch it in groups of 50 targets. These numbers can be tweaked to better match our infra capacity:

0 - 150: No impact
150 - 250: Slight reduction
250+: Lowest priority

Cocoon can leave a comment on the PR as an FYI that the user is being throttled for using too much capacity.

Status: shipped/closed

### Use case

Hi thanks for the great framework! When developing https://github.com/fzyzcjy/flutter_rust_bridge v2, we are having the following scenario: We need to compile some Rust code into WASM, which is used by Flutter code.

text:
Feature Request #139012: Support `DismissDirection` in `SnackBarThemeData`

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Currently, `SnackBar` supports `DismissDirection` as a widget parameter. However it is not supported in `SnackBarThemeData` and cannot be added to the overall app theme. For comparison, `SnackBarBehavior` can be passed in both `SnackBar` and `SnackBarThemeData`.

This means whenever I create a SnackBar, I need to explicitely specify the `DismissDirection` in every SnackBar instantiation when it would be easier to just define in my app theme. 

### Proposal

Add a parameter for specifying `DismissDirection` in `SnackBarThemeData`.

```
SnackBarThemeData(
    dismissDirection: DismissDirection.horizontal,
)
```

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #139350: Custom install fonts and other files

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Is it possible to do a local installation of all external files that are downloaded from the server, to your server, or directly to the asset?
This is necessary for two cases:
1) If you need to install the site (web version) on a secure network without access to the external Internet
2) Long time downloading a file from the server. If you put the file on the local network, the download will probably happen faster.

[Example file](https://fonts.gstatic.com/s/notosanssc/v36/k3kCo84MPvpLmixcA63oeAL7Iqp5IZJF9bmaG9_FnYxNbPzS5HE.ttf
)

![image](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/92320184/2dfcb19c-eba1-453c-998d-06f4017c61fe)


### Proposal

Local install fonts

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

Go router currently provides the StatefulShellRoute.indexedStack() constructor. It works well when you are working with any index-based navbar and want to keep the widget state. But there is no clean way to dispose or reload the state once you are going to another location / coming back to the old location. The lack of methods to dispose the state is a problem with big apps especially.

In order to achieve a clean dispose-architecture, you can use the normal ShellRoute, but when working with a NavigationRail e.g. you have much boilerplate code to manage for initial selection, pushing routes if the selection changes and so on. 

### Proposal

A constructor like "ShellRoute.indexed()" would be great for this use case, with getters like in "StatefulShellRoute.indexedStack()" (getter for index and the ability to go to a route with an index).

If I'm overlooking another way of achieving this in a clean manner, please let me know.

text:
Feature Request #139395: Improve and fix MenuAnchor/MenuItemButton UX for desktop

MenuAnchor for Material 3 is great for contextual menus but there are some minor issues related to accessibility. Specifically, keyboard navigation does not auto-select an element when opening the menu. This causes some UX confusion where the user has to navigate further to access the menu he or she just opened, which in my case is not user friendly.

Setup:
- Material 3
- Windows 10 22H2
- Flutter 3.16.2

Relevant PR:
- [ ] https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/139396 MenuItemButton doesn't have an `autofocus` attribute, thereby making it harder to navigate with the keyboard. 


Thanks Flutter Team!

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #139692: [macOS] Add mouse cursor countingUpHand, countingDownHand, and countingUpAnDownHand

All system mouse cursors from macOS's `CoreCursor` are planned to be included except for the following three:

* kCountingUpHandCursor
* kCountingDownHandCursor
* kCountingUpAndDownHandCursor

These cursors look like a hand counting with fingers, animating from 1 to 5 or backwards. 

There are two reason they are not included:
* During testing I found that they do not animate unless the mouse is moving. (I might be wrong, since my testing app is throwing errors.)
* They're exclusively present on macOS and I'm not aware of any uses.

If you find this cursor necessary, comment below with a use case (or better, a name) and we can discuss adding this cursor.

Related:
* https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/61397

Status: shipped/closed

This cause a delay, which is not a good UX

text:
Feature Request #139929: Deeplinking web validator (Android)

A deep link is a special type of URL that takes users directly to a specific page or section within an app. It contains a scheme, host, and path to open the app and navigate to the desired location. 

Deep links allow end-users to easily navigate to the relevant content in the mobile app from a mobile website. Because mobile apps generally provide better user experience, users who’re directed to an app are twice more likely to convert (e.g. make a purchase) than users who are directed to the mobile web. 

However, deeplinking setup is complicated and often error-prone. We hope to improve this deeplinking implementation experience by first providing a validator to help developers determine if their implementation is accurate. 

In Flutter applications, a deeplink setup consists of app setup and web setup. Assuming a developer starts with a fresh Flutter application, the app setup includes the adding certain settings or tags in native Android/iOS files and the dart code to handle incoming urls. The web setup involves maintaining a website that hosts a file to associate its web domain to a flutter app.

This particular issue tracks the web setup validation for Android app...

Status: shipped/closed

Feature Request #140046: Allow to customize SearchView measurements

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I would like to be able to edit the Search view measurements in the `SearchView` widget. Specifically i would like to edit the height, which is currently fixed to 56 (see image below). 
I could not find a way to do so, and I think it is not currently possible, but please let me know if I overlooked something, 

![image](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/62503755/a22265f7-470c-4b60-b675-85d6993770fc)

`

### Proposal

The Search view measurements described in https://m3.material.io/components/search/specs should be editable when defining the `SearchView` or `SearchAnchor` widget.

text:
Feature Request #140271: TapRegion has no clear way to differentiate between different gestures

### Use case

By default, the on screen keyboard is dismissed by non-mobile touch events, such as a click.
It is quite common to want similar behavior on mobile. That can be done by calling `unfocus` on the text fields `FocusNode` inside the `onTapCallback`. That does however mean that both taps and scrolls are handled the same, since `TapRegion` uses `PointerDownEvent` as a trigger for `onTapOutside`. (Contradictory to what the name might suggest. Compare with `onTap` and `onTapDown` in `GestureDetector`)

Having the ability to differentiate between scroll and tap would give developers the ability to close the text field on a tap, but keep it open when scrolling.

Attached below is a video of the Google Home app on Android, showing the behavior that I'm looking for.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/47989573/77d26c16-5359-44ae-8002-8e30ff88f8e8

### Proposal

`TapRegion`, should have more parameters to differentiate between different gestures, similar to `GestureDetector`. This would be a breaking change, but `onTapOutside` should behave like `onTap` of `GestureDetector`, and a new parameter with the name `onTapDownOutside` should be added that has the current...

Community support: 4 reactions (4 upvotes)

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #140599: Show scrollbar on 2d scroll in ListView without Width

### Use case

this use case is mainly on the web and desktop,
let's say you have a 2D scroll and on the vertical scroll you have a wild row 
what to expect is the scrollbar should be visible on the edge of the  vertical scroll  not at the end of it 
so you don't need to slide horizontally to see the vertical scrollbar:

Here is an example:
Btw I can't just make 2D scroll to listview without giving it a width

```
    return SingleChildScrollView(
      scrollDirection: Axis.horizontal,
      child: SizedBox(
        width: 3000,
        child: ListView(
          children: [
            for (var i = 0; i < 100; i++)
              Padding(
                padding: const EdgeInsets.all(8.0),
                child: Container(
                  height: 100,
                  width: 100,
                  color: [Colors.red, Colors.green, Colors.blue][i % 3],
                  child: Text("data $i"),
                ),
              ),
          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
```


### Proposal


https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/36929077/4e25e37a-bff2-40d4-9f41-8d9031746851


The vertical should be visible without the need to scroll horizontally

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #140622: Rename MemoryAllocations to FlutterMemoryAllocations.

**Why?**

Disposables in Dart cannot use MemoryAllocations in Flutter. So, to be leak trackable they need a dart-only class.
See detains in [the document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ireWmX4J5Jc6oDO1PmAXPlLdiAlCHvTz8SVC_Sg9KM8/edit?resourcekey=0-yODb_SAStMgMefYpQeX8uQ#bookmark=id.e3vlg7f7t1l).

**Why now?**

1. We want early adopters of leak tracker (for example, package owners) to use the class with the right name, so that they do not deal with renaming later.
2. It will take time for the renaming to land to flutter stable.

**Steps:**

- [x] Add FlutterMemoryAllocations and deprecate MemoryAllocations in Flutter
- [x] Document breaking change in Flutter: https://github.com/flutter/website/pull/10021
- [x] Update leak tracker to use FlutterMemoryAllocations
- [x] Roll leak tracker to G3
- [x] Upgrade flutter to new leak tracker
- [ ] Remove MemoryAllocations from Flutter


Community support: 2 reactions (2 upvotes)

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #140656: Display information-type of each column in `flutter emulators` command.

### Use case

There can be a table-header which can show what type of information each column represents. Just for better understanding.

### Proposal

Here's the output of a `flutter emulators` command. There can be a table-header which can show what type of information each column represents.

### Before

```
4 available emulators:

apple_ios_simulator      • iOS Simulator          • Apple  • ios
Pixel_2_API_34        • Pixel 2 API 34         • Google • android
Pixel_7_Pro_API_34      • Pixel 7 Pro API 34       • Google • android
Resizable_Experimental_API_33 • Resizable (Experimental) API 33 • Generic • android
```

### After

```
4 available emulators:

ID • Name • Manufacturer • Platform Type

apple_ios_simulator      • iOS Simulator          • Apple  • ios
Pixel_2_API_34        • Pixel 2 API 34         • Google • android
Pixel_7_Pro_API_34      • Pixel 7 Pro API 34       • Google • android
Resizable_Experimental_API_33 • Resizable (Experimental) API 33 • Generic • android
```

---

Similar Issue for `flutter devices` command: #138983 

Status: shipped/closed

text:
Feature Request #140692: [l10n] Allow `l10n.yaml` to specify line length formatting

### Use case

Hey Flutter Team, 

`flutter run` is breaking our localization files formatting regardless of configuration in `l10n.yaml`. We're using line length 100 in our project. 


- Having `format: true` in `l10n.yaml` will format to line length 80 (default). 
- Having `format: false` in `l10n.yaml` will format to full line length (no boundry) 

We're checking in localization into source code. And unfortunately there is no way to specify the line length that the generated localization files should be in. 

### Proposal

Add a flag to specify line length in  `l10n.yaml`

Community support: 2 reactions (2 upvotes)

Status: shipped/closed

text:
With content-hashing, a given git hash is _no longer_ guaranteed to provide an engine build.

For example, [Linux flutter_release_builder / 1007](https://ci.chromium.org/ui/p/dart-internal/builders/flutter/Linux%20flutter_release_builder/1007/overview) correctly skipped building an engine as unnecessary, as a previous builder built the same (content-hashed) engine. However, this is in conflict with [how we provide `FLUTTER_PREBUILT_ENGINE_VERSION=`, at least in presubmits](https://github.com/flutter/cocoon/blob/b88be3ecf4e49a5a8138940cc2e91e72ba6ca84e/app_dart/lib/src/service/luci_build_service.dart#L286-L293).

From talking to @jtmcdole, we should just be able to stop providing this, at least ... for release builders? Need to double check.
